description_long: This is a traditional stone water well with a circular stone base and a raised stone wall forming the main body. The well is topped with a pitched wooden roof covered in weathered shingles, supported by two sturdy wooden posts. A wooden crank handle is attached to one side, connected to a horizontal spindle and rope mechanism for lowering and raising a wooden bucket. The bucket hangs centrally within the well opening, suspended by a rope. The stonework appears aged and textured, while the wood shows signs of exposure to the elements. The overall construction is robust, designed to provide access to groundwater while protecting the opening from debris.
description: A classic stone water well with a shingled wooden roof, crank handle, and suspended bucket, featuring weathered stone and wood textures.
description_view_0: Front view showing the shingled roof, crank handle on the right, and bucket hanging inside the well.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the pitched roof, wooden support beams, and the crank handle's side profile.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, but with the crank handle on the left and clear view of the bucket and rope.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the wooden support structure and the bucket, with the crank handle less visible.
attribute_well_cover_design: Pitched wooden roof with shingled surface, supported by two wooden posts; no lid or grating over the well opening.
attribute_presence_of_pulley_or_bucket_m: Visible wooden crank handle and spindle with rope and wooden bucket suspended in the well opening.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: Circular stone wall approximately waist-high, forming a protective barrier around the well opening; wooden roof provides overhead protection.
